pi_items.source: GeoServer setup script (setup_geoserver.sh:137) creates workspace 'ne' and publishes layers into it
GeoServer setup script (setup_geoserver.sh:120, 186) imports ne_110m_populated_places.shp to ne_populated_places table and publishes it as layer

GeoServer KML Regionation documentation (https://geoserver.geosolutionsgroup.com/edu/en/google_earth/regionation.html) confirms external-sorting as valid regionateStrategy: 'Creates a temporary auxiliary database within GeoServer. It takes slightly extra time to build the index upon first request.'

Natural Earth 110m populated places dataset schema includes pop_max attribute representing metropolitan area population. Confirmed in multiple sources: Natural Earth documentation and Natural Earth GitHub repository (https://github.com/nvkelso/natural-earth-vector) showing pop_max field in ne_110m_populated_places.shp

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real, public data from the Natural Earth project (110m populated places dataset). The GeoServer environment is configured with workspace 'ne' containing the layer 'ne_populated_places' imported from the official Natural Earth ne_110m_populated_places shapefile. The metadata correctly specifies 'external-sorting' as a valid KML regionation strategy and 'pop_max' as a valid population-based attribute field from the Natural Earth dataset for use in regionation. All four metadata claims are accurate and correspond to real geospatial data and GeoServer configuration features.
